new_capabilities: Explicit --function windows can now be scanned without requiring the root field load inside the same function.
--seed-register and repeated --seed-path offsets seed split handlers with a known object path at function entry.
Seeded traces preserve the older root-offset workflow while adding split state-machine support for continuation bodies.
tooling_milestone.why_added: The 0x14029E7C6 authCode producer is reached as a split handler: RDI already points at the controller child, so a same-function client +0x198D0 root scan cannot see the object lifetime. Seeding RDI as client +0x198D0 -> +0x110 makes that path native to the tool.

seed.meaning: RDI is treated as the controller child object previously reached through the client login-controller path.

key_events.interpretation: Reads child +0x60, matching the prior layout where the controller child carries the client +0x536B8 service source.
Follows child +0x60 +0x0 before the service-slot path used by the OTL dispatcher.
Writes the controller child state word on the early dispatch path.
Writes the controller child state word on the ShellExecute fallback path.
Passes the child +0x38 slot address to the copy/build helper, strengthening the authCode continuation-slot mapping.
Sets controller child state 0x14 after the +0x38 authCode/continuation string is built.

confirmed: The seeded trace reproduces the manual finding that 0x14029E7C6 operates on the same controller child path as earlier child +0x110 analysis.
The function uses child +0x60 for the service path and child +0x38 for the authCode/OTL continuation string destination.
The state field at child +0x0 is written before/after the authCode path, including final state 0x14.
reversing_result.bounded_inference: 0x14029E7C6 remains the strongest mapped producer for the controller child +0x110 +0x38 authCode/OTL continuation string. The new tool support lowers uncertainty around object identity because the same event set now comes from a reusable seeded trace rather than a one-off manual walk.
reversing_result.opennxt_relevance: For OpenNXT 947, this keeps separating web/OTL handoff behavior from the main game-login socket: the server work should focus on protocol compatibility, while this path explains how the native client stages or consumes an authCode/OTL continuation around external URL/service dispatch.

next_targets: Trace the outer split state-machine block around 0x14029E538 to name the predecessor that seeds RDI before 0x14029E7C6.
Add a second seed or argument-origin mode so incoming RDX +0x80/+0x88 and +0xD0 in 0x14029E7C6 can be mapped to its request/event object.
Use seeded traces on neighboring controller-child handlers to build a state-value table for [child+0x0].
